entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop being studied for aflatoxin contamination and the efficacy of GAPs.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: A qualitative issue in groundnuts that poses a threat to food safety.

entities.name: GOOD AGRICULTURAL PRACTICES
entities.description: A set of practices including manure, gypsum, irrigation, and proper drying to reduce aflatoxin.

entities.name: FARMYARD MANURE
entities.description: Application of 5 t/ha as part of GAPs.

entities.name: GYPSUM
entities.description: Application of 500 kg/ha as part of GAPs.

entities.name: PROTECTIVE IRRIGATION
entities.description: Irrigation applied at 90 days after sowing as part of GAPs.

entities.name: DRYING OF PODS ON TARPAULINS
entities.description: Post-harvest drying method used in farmers' fields as part of GAPs.

entities.name: ON-FARM DEMONSTRATIONS
entities.description: An educative tool used to demonstrate the efficacy and cost-effectiveness of GAPs.

entities.name: FARMERS' PRACTICES
entities.description: The traditional cultivation methods used as a control for comparison with GAPs.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: The fungus responsible for pre-harvest kernel infection in groundnuts.

entities.name: KERNEL INFECTION
entities.description: Infection of groundnut kernels by Aspergillus flavus.

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of groundnut pods produced, used as a performance metric.

entities.name: COST OF CULTIVATION
entities.description: The economic expenditure required for growing groundnuts.

entities.name: FOOD SAFETY
entities.description: The condition of food being safe for consumption, threatened by aflatoxins.
